messages.content: Build an Effect v3 program that races a primary HTTP fetch against a cached-value effect using Effect.raceWith. Whichever wins, interrupt the loser explicitly and log which branch produced the value. Typed errors, no `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to race two effects while running a compensating action on the loser? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Effect.raceWith differs from Effect.race in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 function raceWithMetrics(a, b) that races two effects. Constraints: use Effect.raceWith, interrupt the loser in BOTH branches, and return the Exit unchanged. No `any`.
Add explicit loser-cleanup logging to this v3 race so that whichever fiber loses prints "interrupted" before the winner's exit propagates: `const raced = Effect.race(a, b)`.

Build an Effect v3 raceFirstSuccess helper that races effect `a` against effect `b` using raceWith: if the first completer succeeds, interrupt the loser and return it; if it fails, wait for the other. Typed errors.

Build an Effect v3 validator for a SignUp form (name, email, age) that accumulates ALL field errors using Effect.validateAll. Each validator returns Effect<Field, string>. Do not short-circuit on the first failure.
What's the Effect v3 way to run three validators concurrently and return every error instead of the first one? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly what fp-ts Applicative-validation buys you over a monadic chain, then show an fp-ts 2.x implementation that accumulates Either errors into a ReadonlyNonEmptyArray<string>.

Write an Effect v3 function validateConfig(keys: string[]) that checks every key against an env-lookup. Constraints: use Effect.partition (NOT validateAll), return both the failures and the successes as separate arrays. No `any`.

Build an fp-ts 2.x validateUser(raw: unknown) that uses Applicative validation to collect errors from three independent field validators, producing Either<ReadonlyNonEmptyArray<string>, User>. Strict types.
Build an Effect v3 worker pool that processes a list of URLs with a concurrency limit of 4 using Effect.makeSemaphore and Effect.forEach. Each fetch is typed. No `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to cap concurrency at N for a forEach? Show a minimal example using makeSemaphore.
Explain briefly why a shared Semaphore beats per-call concurrency when two forEach loops must share a global budget, then show a working Effect v3 implementation.

Write an Effect v3 runPool<A, E, B>(items: A[], f, n: number) that runs `f` over every item with at most `n` concurrent executions. Constraints: use makeSemaphore, return Effect<B[], E>, preserve the input order. No `any`.
Build an Effect v3 image-thumbnailer pool: given a list of image ids, generate thumbnails at most 8 at a time, collect results into a Map<id, thumbUrl>. Use makeSemaphore + forEach. Typed errors.

Build an Effect v3 bulkhead that pairs a bounded Queue<Job> (capacity 100) with a Semaphore (permits 4). Producers offer jobs; a pool of 4 worker fibers take from the queue and process them. Typed, no `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to combine a bounded Queue with a Semaphore to get both back-pressure AND concurrency capping? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly what a bulkhead pattern is and why a bounded Queue + Semaphore together are stronger than either alone, then show an Effect v3 implementation.

Write an Effect v3 makeBulkhead(cap, workers) that returns an offer(job) function. Constraints: bounded Queue for back-pressure, Semaphore for concurrent handler cap, handler signature Job => Effect<void>. No `any`.
Build an Effect v3 webhook ingester: a bounded Queue of events (cap 500) feeds a Semaphore-gated worker (3 concurrent). On shutdown, drain the queue before returning. Typed events.
Build an Effect v3 Stream pipeline that consumes keystrokes from a source stream, debounces by 200ms, and searches against a typed API. No `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to debounce a Stream of events by 500ms so only the last value in a burst survives? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly the difference between debounce and throttle as Stream operators in Effect v3, then show a working example that uses both in a pipeline.

Write an Effect v3 Stream pipeline that consumes an incoming event Stream<Cmd>, throttles to at most 10 per second using Stream.throttle with token-bucket semantics. Constraints: use the shape strategy, preserve the event type. No `any`.
Build an Effect v3 Stream that batches an event source into chunks of up to 20 items or every 100ms (whichever comes first) using Stream.groupedWithin. Typed elements, no `any`.
Build an Effect v3 circuit breaker backed by Ref<State> with states closed, open, halfOpen. After 3 failures trip to open for 1s, then allow one probe (half-open). Typed, no `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to short-circuit calls for a cooldown window after N failures? Show a minimal Ref-backed gate.
Explain briefly the three circuit-breaker states (closed, open, half-open) and why half-open exists, then show a typed Ref-backed Effect v3 implementation.
Write an Effect v3 wrapCircuit(eff, threshold, cooldownMs) that returns the same effect protected by a Ref-backed breaker. Constraints: tagged error BreakerOpen, typed states, no `any`.
Build an Effect v3 Cache that looks up User by id, caps at 500 entries with a 10s TTL. Lookup function fetches from an HTTP service. Typed errors, no `any`.
What's the Effect v3 way to memoise an async lookup with a 30s TTL? Show a minimal example using Cache.make.
Explain briefly what TTL and capacity mean in Effect v3's Cache.make and how they interact, then show a working typed cache over a DB lookup.
Write an Effect v3 makeTtlCache<K, V, E>(capacity, ttl, lookup) returning Effect<Cache>. Constraints: use Cache.make, preserve typed errors, no `any`.
Build an Effect v3 pricing cache with a 5 second TTL backed by Cache.make. On miss, it calls an HTTP pricing API that may fail with PricingUnavailable. Typed.
